summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The OutputCommitter class is part of the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework and is responsible for managing the commit of task output for a Map-Reduce job.

CORE LOGIC:
The OutputCommitter class provides methods for setting up and cleaning up job output, setting up task output, checking if a task needs a commit, committing task output, discarding task output, and recovering task output in case of job restart.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The OutputCommitter class takes inputs such as job context and task attempt context, and it produces outputs such as job status and task commit status.

DEPENDENCIES:
The OutputCommitter class depends on other classes such as JobContext, TaskAttemptContext, and JobStatus.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: The TaskAttemptContext interface provides methods for setting and getting the status of a task attempt, as well as methods for accessing counters.

CORE LOGIC: The interface does not contain any core logic.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS: The interface provides methods for setting and getting the status of a task attempt, as well as methods for accessing counters.

DEPENDENCIES: The TaskAttemptContext interface extends the JobContext interface and implements the Progressable interface. It also imports the TaskAttemptID class from the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class is a special committer which creates the factory for the committer and runs off that.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic is the delegation of all commit protocol methods and accessors to the wrapped committer.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The class takes an output path and a task context as inputs, and it has an output path and a task context as outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:
The class depends on the PathOutputCommitterFactory, PathOutputCommitter, JobContext, TaskAttemptContext, IOException, and other classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- commitJob: moves files from work directory to job output directory
- abortJob: deletes the temporary directory, including all of the work directories
- setupTask: no task setup required
- commitTask: moves files from work directory to job output directory
- abortTask: deletes the work directory
- needsTaskCommit: checks if the task wrote any files in the work directory
- isRecoverySupported: indicates that task recovery is supported
- isCommitJobRepeatable: indicates that commitJob is repeatable
- recoverTask: recovers task output from the previous attempt

CORE LOGIC:
- commitJob: uses algorithmVersion to determine whether to directly merge files from taskAttemptPath to output directory or rename the taskAttemptPath to committedTaskPath
- commitTask: uses algorithmVersion to determine whether to directly merge files from taskAttemptPath to output directory or rename the taskAttemptPath to committedTaskPath
- abortTask: deletes the work directory
- needsTaskCommit: checks if the task wrote any files in the work directory
- isRecoverySupported: indicates that task recovery is supported
- isCommitJobRepeatable: indicates that commitJob is repeatable
- recoverTask: recovers task output from the previous attempt

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- commitJob: takes JobContext as input and does not produce any output
- abortJob: takes JobContext as input and does not produce any output
- setupTask: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output
- commitTask: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output
- abortTask: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output
- needsTaskCommit: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output
- isRecoverySupported: takes no input and does not produce any output
- isCommitJobRepeatable: takes JobContext as input and does not produce any output
- recoverTask: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output

DEPENDENCIES:
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.FileOutputCommitter
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.JobContext
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.JobStatus
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptContext
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptID
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.FileSystem
- org

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

Creates a FileOutputCommitter, always.

CORE LOGIC:

None visible.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

Input: Path outputPath, TaskAttemptContext context
Output: FileOutputCommitter

DEPENDENCIES:

org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.FileOutputCommitter
org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptContext
org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
java.io.IOException

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The NamedCommitterFactory class is a factory that creates any named committer identified in the option org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.NamedCommitterFactory.NAMED_COMMITTER_CLASS.

CORE LOGIC:
- The createOutputCommitter method creates an instance of the named committer class using reflection.
- The loadCommitterClass method loads the committer class named in the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.NamedCommitterFactory.NAMED_COMMITTER_CLASS option.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The createOutputCommitter method takes a Path and a TaskAttemptContext as input and returns a PathOutputCommitter.
- The loadCommitterClass method takes a JobContext as input and returns a Class<? extends PathOutputCommitter>.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The NamedCommitterFactory class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configuration, org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path, org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.JobContext, and org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptContext classes.
- The createOutputCommitter method depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.PathOutputCommitter class.
- The loadCommitterClass method depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.FileOutputCommitter class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: commits files specified in job output directory
CORE LOGIC: removes any committed output for a task
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: data flowing in and out
DEPENDENCIES: key imported, parent, or collaborator classes visible in this chunk

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The PathOutputCommitterFactory class is a factory for committers implementing the PathOutputCommitter methods, and can be used from FileOutputFormat.

CORE LOGIC:
The algorithm for creating a committer factory is as follows:
1. If an explicit committer factory is named, it is used.
2. The output path is examined. If it is non-null and there is an explicit schema for that filesystem, its factory is instantiated.
3. Otherwise, an instance of FileOutputCommitter is created.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The createOutputCommitter method takes a Path and a TaskAttemptContext as inputs and returns a new committer.

DEPENDENCIES:
The PathOutputCommitterFactory class depends on the following classes:
- Configured: for configuration-related functionality
- PathOutputCommitter: for committer functionality
- FileOutputCommitter: for default committer functionality
- TaskAttemptContext: for task attempt context functionality
- Configuration: for configuration settings
- Path: for path-related functionality
- IOException: for handling input/output errors

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This is the committer factory to register as the source of committers
for the job/filesystem schema.

CORE LOGIC:
The factory checks the schema of the output path and fails fast if it is not
supported.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The factory creates a committer instance for the output path and task context.

DEPENDENCIES:
The factory depends on the internal constants and the manifest committer class.
